concepts.finding:
A Cypher query finds similar articles using a precomputed similarity
metric
MATCH (a1:Article)-[r:similar]-(recommendation)
WHERE a1.url = $url1
RETURN recommendation,
       r.metric AS similarity_metric
ORDER BY similarity_metric DESC LIMIT 5

rules can vary in complexity but generally will be based on exact matches (useful

similarity, value approximation, etc.).

concepts.finding:
Linking categories in Cypher
MATCH (e:Entity { name: $entityname })
MATCH (c:Category)
WHERE e.name IN c.alts
WITH e, collect(c) AS candidate_cats
WITH e, selection_logic(candidate_cats) AS selected_cat
MERGE (e)-[:references]->(selected_cat)

concepts.finding:
Calculating the Wu and Palmer similarity metric in Python
from nltk.corpus import wordnet as wn
dog = wn.synset('dog.n.01')
lion = wn.synset('lion.n.01')
print(dog.definition())
print(lion.definition())
print(wn.wup_similarity(dog, lion))
